title: Polymer Nanocapsules Entrapping Metal Nanoparticles (US 9,248,441 B2)
expected_content_elements: Front-page metadata (title, inventors, assignee, patent number, dates)
key_details: Invention describes metal nanoparticles (e.g., silver, platinum, palladium, gold) entrapped in polymer nanocapsules.
Claims cover composition of nanocapsules and methods for synthesizing them, often using free-radical polymerization in lipid bilayers.
Applications mentioned include catalysis, sensing, imaging, and therapeutic delivery.
semantic_themes: Patent claim structure and legal language
layout_features: Two-column legal/technical format is possible in parts; most sections are single-column USPTO style text.
Section labels in uppercase (e.g., BACKGROUND OF THE INVENTION, SUMMARY OF THE INVENTION).
Claims are numbered with nested sub-clauses.
Frequent figure references in all caps (e.g., FIG. 1).
images_and_diagrams_description: Contains several figures: e.g., FIG. 1 schematic of nanocapsule synthesis; FIGS. 2A–2D TEM and XRD imagery; FIG. 3 spectral plots (UV, plasmon resonance). Diagrams include axis labels and figure captions.
tables_description: No traditional tables; data appears mainly in figure plots and in prose.
extraction_checklist: Did the extractor preserve the full abstract as a single coherent block?
Are section titles (BACKGROUND, SUMMARY, DETAILED DESCRIPTION, CLAIMS) correctly segmented?
Are claim numbers and sub-clauses preserved with correct numbering and hierarchy?
Are figure captions and references (e.g., FIG. 2A–2D) captured without losing letters or numbers?
Are chemical and measurement symbols (e.g., nm, kV, °C) preserved without corruption?
